jobs.description:
Are you ready to shape the future of enterprise AI and redefine how customers experience the next generation of Alexa? We're seeking a Senior B2B Product Marketing Manager to collaborate with our world-class enterprise customers, helping them leverage both voice technology and Generative AI capabilities to transform their customer experience, drive revenue and reduce costs.

The ideal candidate will have strong storytelling skills and a track record of leading successful programs (including ownership of messaging, positioning, and GTM strategy). They will demonstrate strengths in working collaboratively across organizations, managing key milestones in an ambiguous, fast-moving environment—while keeping senior stakeholders updated on progress.

Key job responsibilities
Develop and execute go-to-market strategies in collaboration with stakeholders across the organization, including Product, Design, Marketing, PR, and Business Development teams

Lead product positioning, messaging and storytelling that effectively drives thought-leadership and communicates value

Craft and develop high-impact content (presentations, whitepapers, webinars, customer success stories, blog, emails, product videos) that supports the full enterprise buying journey

Develop and maintain sales enablement content to accelerate pipeline growth and increase sales productivity

Conduct research to deepen understanding of our target personas and industries to continuously refine our GTM strategies and product roadmap

About the team
Alexa Enterprise’s charter is to build and enable indispensable, frustration-free, Alexa experiences for enterprise customers and partners worldwide. We build a portfolio of products that empower developers and enterprise companies to use voice and touch to support their staff and delight their customers. Our products have been featured in publications such as CNET and Forbes and Amazon has celebrated our progress in its quarterly earnings highlights. Customers have told us: 'You have saved lives' and ‘helped our residents connect with their families.’ We are a diverse and collaborative team united by our mission to make Alexa an indispensable part of customers' lives.

jobs.description:
“Going from Zero to One is the greatest improvement possible, greater than going from one to 10 or even from one to 100. To go from zero to one is to bring something into existence and therefore is the essence of true innovation”- Peter Thiel

Are you someone who is passionate about thriving with such Zero to One challenges & love building new strategic and tech-driven businesses? Join the Smart Commerce team as Marketing Leader. The vision of Smart Commerce team is to digitize brand owners and selling partners by providing a suite of product offerings across the business' lifecycle. Our products enable SMBs to start, manage & grow their D2C business. Our end to end offerings from website builder to checkout product to order management system along with aided services will supercharge seller success as they thrive in today’s omni channel landscape.

The ideal candidate is expected to have significant marketing experience in leading teams, creating strategy and delivering results in a high stakes environment. The candidate will think and act both strategically and tactically: from strategic planning to detailed execution. He /She will lead data-driven plans, bring thought leadership across marketing initiatives & execute them through the team. The candidate is also expected to work with senior cross-functional stakeholders and should have the ability to influence without authority. We are looking for a relentless, customer-obsessed, and business-minded marketer with a penchant for invention, operational excellence and delivering results.


Key job responsibilities
Key job responsibilities
• Lead the overall marketing strategy across traffic, awareness, acquisition & brand marketing for Smart Commerce offerings
• Lead a team of high-performing marketers across performance marketing, brand, growth, lifecycle marketing and community management.
• Work towards improving our understanding of the target audience through customer research, usability studies and data deep-dives
• Establish, implement and measure the effectiveness of Seller acquisition initiatives to drive acquisition of new sellers.
• Develop the right brand strategy for overall Smart commerce offerings & establish through website, social, events & PR
• Closely collaborate with product team to influence the product roadmap through marketing insights


• BASIC QUALIFICATIONS
- 7+ years of professional core marketing experience
- Experience in managing teams
- Experience using data and metrics to drive improvements
- Experience driving direction and alignment with cross-functional teams
- Experience guiding creative input, development and execution from concept to completion
PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS
- Experience in developing marketing charters including branding, PR, content, awareness in nascent programs/start ups

jobs.description:
Amazon Worldwide Operations is looking for Program Managers to own strategic, tactical, cross-functional and technology projects and programs within Amazon Transportation Services. These roles require scoping and creating project plans, developing processes, coordinating and driving execution, and communicating to senior management on status, risks and process/product changes. Cross-team coordination, project management and executive presentation skills are essential.

***This role can be based in any Canadian Amazon location***



Key job responsibilities
• Be a network level Subject Matter Expert (SME) on the CMMS (Computerized Maintenance Management System), train and assist the local Reliability Maintenance Engineering Planner and support network initiatives.
• Drive Global standards roll-out in cross-country initiatives and participate in Global led projects.
• Be a CMMS key user and drive programs to roll out new functionality, procedures, scheduling or reports. Monitor standards implementation through reporting and audits.
• Analyze and improve forecasting of preventative maintenance routines to make equipment maintenance less intrusive and more effective.
• Compile equipment information, performance metrics and machine histories and make recommendations for improvements based on their findings to the entire network.
• Work with the Global Reliability Maintenance Engineering network and initiatives on cost reduction and process improvement for spare parts management, equipment maintenance and maintenance routines.
• Ability to Travel up to 50%

A day in the life
• Working in a team where talent and potential have more value than paper-based qualifications
• Global opportunities to develop in role and choose long term career paths
• Working at the cusp of high-speed automated logistics technology
• Finding and conquering emerging challenges as the company grows and scales rapidly
• Working in data driven environment where accuracy and clarity is prized above personal opinion

Amazon offers a full range of benefits for you and eligible family members, including domestic partners and their children. Benefits can vary by location, the number of regularly scheduled hours you work, length of employment, and job status such as seasonal or temporary employment. The benefits that generally apply to regular, full-time employees include:
1. Medical, Dental, and Vision Coverage
2. Maternity and Parental Leave Options
3. Paid Time Off (PTO)
4. 401(k) Plan

If you are not sure that every qualification on the list above describes you exactly, we'd still love to hear from you! At Amazon, we value people with unique backgrounds, experiences, and skillsets. If you’re passionate about this role and want to make an impact on a global scale, please apply!

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Environmental Safety degree or equivalent Master certification
- International safety certification (NEBOSH)
- Demonstrated extensive experience in safety management within construction environments
- Bilingual proficiency in German and English
jobs.description:
At Amazon, we're working to be the most customer-centric company on earth. To get there, we need exceptionally talented, bright, and driven people. If you'd like to help us build the place to find and buy anything online, this is your chance to make history. Amazon Construction Health & Safety (H&S) mission is to provide tools, resources, and environment that support safe, efficient, and healthy behaviors across Amazon construction projects. This individual will play a critical role to ensure the implementation of high standards of safety and adherence across all construction projects in MEU (Germany, Poland, Czech Republic, Slovakia & Austria). Establishing H&S compliance across existing and new contractual agreements with our vendors. The successful candidate will support the delivery of the International H&S, reporting to the International Field Lead
The role will require knowledge of local Construction safety standards, regulations and behavioral safety habits and understanding of Commercial client obligations. Emphasis on sound safety principles, basic construction knowledge and the ability to engage with cross functional teams, transforming this knowledge to develop Standard Operating procedures, adapt existing safety standards to meet future requirements and enhance safety compliance processes, scaling to support MEU but Thinking Big to support the globally. This individual will be accountable for the region to meet Safety Targets; working through vendor MBR's, managing local Health and Safety Consultant network, increasing the adoption of BOLT (Digital Safety Mgmt. System) monitoring adherence of Safe Methods of work, compliance to BOLT inspections/audits, delivering high level of safety reporting transparency, whilst driving Safety culture for the region. Utilizing BOLT, the MEU Safety Manager will analyze data to identify emerging trends, implementing mitigation strategies as required & working with Construction teams to inform the of best practices and lessons learnt as applicable.
The individual will provide a functional working link to critical customer including: Design & Construction, Operations Engineering, Start-up, Launch, Reliability Maintenance Engineering, Procurement, Real Estate and Workplace Health & Safety.

Would you like an opportunity to travel and work in multiple countries and cultures? The role will be based in Germany or Poland with 40% of time traveling either domestically or to other EMEA



Key job responsibilities
• Support construction and program managers (CM / PMs) to establish and maintain an effective working relationship with developers, general contractors and subcontractors in relation to construction H&S;
• Support GES CM / PMs in follow-up & drive accountability related to incidents and near misses;
• Support GES CM / PMs and H&S SMEs with external H&S Consultant appointments;
• Conduct reactive and pre-arranged site visits support, guide and advise the Project Team and/or participate in other site-based activities;
• Provide timely assistance to the Project Teams to ensure compliance with local legislation and H&S standards, tools, process and procedure;
• Interrogate safety data metrics to establish trends, safety concerns to develop and implement resolution / mitigation and influence a safety culture;
• Prepare safety metrics analysis by Business Unit / Region and present Safety Weekly Business Reviews;
• Attend / support periodic safety business reviews, audits, and inspections to ensure H&S compliance. Evaluate performance, identify corrective action, and implement follow up assessments;
• Where applicable, attend and participate in virtual and face-to-face GES H&S Team meetings and periodic business reviews;
• Ensure a data-oriented assistance approach throughout the overall project lifecycle;
• Collaborate with the GES H&S Field Assurance Team to ensure the amazon construction operations meets the country specific H&S requirements throughout MEU;
• Support internal GES H&S Team SMEs for continuous improvement;
• Support supply chain Technical Vendor Management and Global Procurement Organization with safety data analysis, safety business reviews and evaluation of new vendors in relation to H&S

The minimum base pay salary for this position is listed below. As a total compensation company, Amazon's package may include further pay components such as a sign on bonus and the eligibility to participate in a restricted stock unit scheme operated independently by Amazon.com Inc. in USA. Your recruiting team will share final base pay and any applicable total compensation elements depending on skills and requirements.

Company discretionary, supplementary benefits apply subject to further terms, including pension plans, health insurance, life and disability insurance, business travel insurance, MultiSport card, discounts for meals at the company’s canteen, employee discount code and program (EDC/Extras), and the Employee Assistance Program (private and confidential counselling and referral service to support health and well-being).

The minimum base pay for this position is PLN 12.116,67 monthly.

- 5+ years of Microsoft Office including Outlook, Word, and Excel experience

- Experience in vendor management
- Experience in a data center or other critical environment
- Experience in data center engineering or operations
- Experience with Microsoft Office including Outlook, Word, and Excel
jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.
You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

The Data Center Capacity Delivery Asia-Pacific Controls Deployment and Service (DCCD APAC CDS) team at Amazon Web Services (AWS) is looking for a Controls Deployment & Service (CDS) Engineers to deliver and support data center building automation systems, such as Building Management Systems (BMS) and Electrical Power Monitoring Systems (EPMS). CDS Engineers work to deploy and service building automation systems, to ensure the health of our data centers. As a CDS Engineer, you may oversee deployment of a new BMS or EPMS as part of a new data center build, deliver projects to upgrade an existing data center owned or leased by Amazon, or deliver projects to upgrade colocated data centers with Amazon hardware. As a CDS Engineer, you may also be required to provide local servicing expertise to our fleet or data centers in your region, and occasionally assist with global support. Successfully working as a CDS Engineer will also require you to partner effectively with the many teams at Amazon that make our data center deployments and operations a success, including Construction, Commissioning, Critical Projects, Data Center Operations, Procurement, Program Management, Design, and Engineering teams, as well as maintaining strong relationships with our vendors.

Key job responsibilities
- Deployment of data center controls, on time, with effective management of vendors to ensure minimal defects at the point of Power and Facilities Hand-Off (PFHO)
- Delivery of critical projects to upgrade data center automation
- Service and support provided to partner teams, providing diagnosis and resolution of Loss of Visibility (LOV), Loss of Resilience (LOR), Large Scale Event (LSE), Critical Scale Event (CSE), and other operations impacting events Resolution of tasks within predefined Service Level Agreements (SLAs)
- Delivery of further initiatives to improve the efficiency of our data centers or our operations procedures

A day in the life
* Join the cloud leader: Customers are choosing AWS over other providers because it has a lot more functionality, the largest and most vibrant community of customers and partners, the most proven operational and security expertise, and the business is innovating at a faster clip—especially in new areas such as Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence, the Internet of Things, Serverless Computing, and custom-designed processors and chips.
* Be at the nexus of action: Your involvement in data center delivery will be mission-critical to the successful deployment of our data centers. You won’t just be observing from the sidelines; you’ll be representing Amazon, building relationships across a wide variety of internal and external stakeholders.
* Build at scale: Build and operate infrastructure at an enormous scale, participating in Amazon’s global presence.
* Your unique background will be an asset: At Amazon, our ability to innovate on behalf of our global customers and communities is dependent upon the skills, perspectives, and knowledge of people from all backgrounds.
* Continuous growth and learning: You may not be an expert in AWS when you join our company, but you will have support to become one. Benefit from direct access to industry-leading specialists, latest training materials, and courses to propel your career.

About the team
About AWS
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.
Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.
Inclusive Team Culture
Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.
Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.
Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

About the team
The Data Center Capacity Delivery Asia-Pacific Controls Deployment and Service (DCCD APAC CDS) team delivers and services the control systems which monitor and ensure the health of our data centers. APAC CDS’ mission is to safely provide our customers with the unmatched reliability, security, and performance that AWS is known for. APAC CDS covers six business domains of deployments, projects, service, colo, on-call support, and management. As Amazonians, we work in a competitive environment. Our customers are always beautifully, wonderfully dissatisfied. Being successful in our roles is not just about doing our jobs well, but also evolving and innovating. Change is the only constant, and applies to our customers, our roles, our team organization, our tools, and our mechanisms.

jobs.description:
With millions of customers visiting us every day to find, discover, and buy products, we’re obsessed with making the shopping experience the best it can be – and advertising is a part of that experience. We strive to make advertising so relevant that customers welcome it – on Amazon, on mobile devices, and across the web. We put the customer at the heart of everything we do.
We are looking for a detail-oriented, analytical, and highly-motivated Ads sales expert to help scale our growing Advertising business. This person will be responsible for all aspects of Brand's advertising journey on Amazon Advertising including sales, on-boarding and launch of ad campaign by developing and leveraging strong relationships with agencies, tech partners, clients and internal teams.
To do this, this person needs to be extremely hands on and have a deep understanding of Amazon's advertising products, sales, operational capabilities, as well as the ability to synthesize analysis into a concise and compelling presentation.

Key job responsibilities
Deliver inputs, Ad products adoption and Ads revenue goals.
Build solid relationships with advertisers, advertising agencies, medium and small brands/ clients, displaying a dedication to delivering first-class service and online advertising solutions.
Identify media agencies and onboard to Amazon Ads network, educate brands on Amazon Advertising offering. Also help analyze campaign performance against key metrics to identify, recommend, and implement optimizations to help Medium/small brands to increase efficiency, drive high renewal rate and meet clients’ KPIs.
Troubleshoot any technical or implementation issues, collaborating with internal teams to develop quick and sound solutions.
Serve as a source of market intelligence for other areas of the Advertising team (e.g., product development, product marketing, pricing) and assist in the development of best practices and operational efficiencies.

jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

The Data Center Capacity Delivery Asia-Pacific Controls Deployment and Service (DCCD APAC CDS) team delivers and services the control systems which monitor and ensure the health of our data centers. APAC CDS’ mission is to safely provide our customers with the unmatched reliability, security, and performance that AWS is known for. APAC CDS covers three core disciplines of deployments (of control systems in new data centers), projects (of controls capital improvement works at live data center sites), and service (of control systems at live data center sites), with additional specializations in the disciplines of colocated sites, systems engineering, controls fleet, on-call support (of data center control systems in the Americas (AMER), Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EMEA), as part of a global “follow-the-sun” support arrangement), and governance.

As Amazonians, we work in a competitive environment. Our customers are always beautifully, wonderfully dissatisfied. Being successful in our roles is not just about doing our jobs well, but also evolving and innovating. Change is the only constant, and applies to our customers, our roles, our team organization, our tools, and our mechanisms.

To be successful in this team, you will also have:
- Ability to thrive at the pace and scale of Amazon
- Ability to work independently and manage multiple competing priorities
- Ability and willingness to collaborate with peers around the globe, which may involve some participation in meetings at odd hours
- Ability and willingness to travel domestically or globally as and when needed
Controls Deployment & Service (CDS) Technicians support data center building automation systems, including Building Management Systems (BMS) and Electrical Power Monitoring Systems (EPMS). In this role, you will deploy and service these critical systems to ensure optimal data center health and performance.

Your responsibilities include deploying new BMS or EPMS installations during data center construction, upgrading existing Amazon-owned or leased facilities, and implementing Amazon hardware in colocated environments. You'll also provide regional servicing expertise to our fleet or data centers in your region, and occasionally support global operations.

Success in this position requires effective collaboration with multiple Amazon teams, including Construction, Commissioning, Critical Projects, Data Center Operations, Procurement, Program Management, Design, and Engineering teams. You'll maintain strong vendor relationships that drive successful deployments and operations.


Key job responsibilities
- Deploy and maintain critical systems: Install, upgrade, and troubleshoot BMS and EPMS systems across multiple data centers, ensuring optimal performance through diagnostics, programming, and hardware maintenance.
- Execute technical projects: Manage installations from server provisioning to sensor calibration, supervise contractor work, and ensure all activities meet AWS safety and quality standards.
- Drive operational excellence: Provide commissioning support, conduct root cause analysis, create system documentation, and follow change management processes to maintain system integrity.
- Enable team success: Mentor facilities teams on system operations, collaborate with vendors on Factory Witnessed Tests, and recommend design improvements based on operational insights.
- Deliver reliable service: Respond to emergencies, manage on-call rotations, oversee service agreements, and prioritize work through ticketing systems to maintain data center availability.

A day in the life
- Join the cloud leader: AWS has the most proven operational and security expertise, and the business is innovating at a faster clip—especially in new areas such as Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence, the Internet of Things, Serverless Computing, and custom-designed processors and chips.
- Be at the nexus of action: Your involvement in data center delivery will be mission-critical to the successful deployment of our data centers. You won’t just be observing from the sidelines; you’ll be representing Amazon, building relationships across a wide variety of internal and external stakeholders.
- Build at scale: Build and operate infrastructure at an enormous scale, participating in Amazon’s global presence.
- Your unique background will be an asset: At Amazon, our ability to innovate on behalf of our global customers and communities is dependent upon the skills, perspectives, and knowledge of people from all backgrounds.
- Continuous growth and learning: You may not be an expert in AWS when you join our company, but you will have support to become one. Benefit from direct access to industry-leading specialists, latest training materials, and courses to propel your career.


About the team
About AWS

Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Controls system: experience building unified automated control system for enterprise level clients, consolidating equipment and subsystems; hardware, software, networking products from multiple vendors, ability to understand PID loop tuning, feedback theory, and industry standard programming: Ladder Logic, Function Block Diagram, Sequential Function Chart, Script Programming Languages
- Project management: ability to understand electrical diagrams, control diagrams, control networks schematics, control panel Bill of Materials (BoM), control panel schematics, experience in inspection, testing and commissioning, ability to manage project scope, schedule, cost, stakeholder expectations, vendor/sub-contractors, multiple concurrent deliverables.
- Language skills: Business level English and intermediate level Japanese
jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.
You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

The Data Center Capacity Delivery Asia-Pacific Controls Deployment and Service (DCCD APAC CDS) team at Amazon Web Services (AWS) is looking for a Controls Deployment & Service (CDS) Engineers to deliver and support data center building automation systems, such as Building Management Systems (BMS) and Electrical Power Monitoring Systems (EPMS). CDS Engineers work to deploy and service building automation systems, to ensure the health of our data centers. As a CDS Engineer, you may oversee deployment of a new BMS or EPMS as part of a new data center build, deliver projects to upgrade an existing data center owned or leased by Amazon, or deliver projects to upgrade colocated data centers with Amazon hardware. As a CDS Engineer, you may also be required to provide local servicing expertise to our fleet or data centers in your region, and occasionally assist with global support. Successfully working as a CDS Engineer will also require you to partner effectively with the many teams at Amazon that make our data center deployments and operations a success, including Construction, Commissioning, Critical Projects, Data Center Operations, Procurement, Program Management, Design, and Engineering teams, as well as maintaining strong relationships with our vendors.

Key job responsibilities
- Deployment of data center controls, on time, with effective management of vendors to ensure minimal defects at the point of Power and Facilities Hand-Off (PFHO)
- Delivery of critical projects to upgrade data center automation
- Service and support provided to partner teams, providing diagnosis and resolution of Loss of Visibility (LOV), Loss of Resilience (LOR), Large Scale Event (LSE), Critical Scale Event (CSE), and other operations impacting events Resolution of tasks within predefined Service Level Agreements (SLAs)
- Delivery of further initiatives to improve the efficiency of our data centers or our operations procedures

About the team
About AWS

Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

About the team
The Data Center Capacity Delivery Asia-Pacific Controls Deployment and Service (DCCD APAC CDS) team delivers and services the control systems which monitor and ensure the health of our data centers. APAC CDS’ mission is to safely provide our customers with the unmatched reliability, security, and performance that AWS is known for. APAC CDS covers six business domains of deployments, projects, service, colo, on-call support, and management. As Amazonians, we work in a competitive environment. Our customers are always beautifully, wonderfully dissatisfied. Being successful in our roles is not just about doing our jobs well, but also evolving and innovating. Change is the only constant, and applies to our customers, our roles, our team organization, our tools, and our mechanisms.

- Knowledge of QA methodology and tools
jobs.description:
If you enjoy working in a fast-paced environment, making a tangible difference in the lives of your customers, and ensuring the best accessibility customer experience on all Amazon Devices through innovative testing mechanisms, this might just be the role for you.

Key job responsibilities
1. Follow SOP and execute test cases prepared for testing software builds on Amazon Devices
2. Raise defects for all failures observed
3. Perform daily quality checks as per established guidelines and report deviations accurately.
4. Responsible for security and maintenance of the company assets, accessories and devices allocated to perform your daily operations.
5. Use software tools for validation, analysis, and data capture on a daily basis.
6. Be comfortable with capturing results, communicating, and escalating failures and providing status reports.

A day in the life
A typical day would have some combination of test execution, issue filing, escalating blocker/critical defects to stakeholders, and testcase optimization. You will also get the opportunity to work on new devices before they are release to customers, and ensure they provide a great experience for accessibility customers.

About the team
The Device Accessibility team is part of the Trust, Privacy & Accessibility (TPA) organization, and is responsible for making Amazon devices accessible to customers with disabilities through hardware design, accessible design of related software, support of assistive technology on device platforms, and communicating with customers with disabilities.
